clinical_coherence.notes: 52F Caucasian with 2 months of progressive word-finding difficulty and personality change. PMH includes HTN, hypothyroidism, mild depression on sertraline. Left temporal mass presenting with aphasia and behavioral changes is clinically coherent. Depression and headaches pre-dating the tumor create diagnostic complexity.

tool_output_consistency.notes: MRI shows left temporal enhancing mass. Advanced imaging consistent with high-grade glioma. The pre-existing depression managed with sertraline adds a layer of diagnostic complexity - personality changes could initially be attributed to worsening depression. All tools concordant with GBM.

ground_truth_quality.notes: ICD C71.9 correct. Left temporal GBM. Differential appropriately includes low-grade glioma and brain metastasis. Depression as confound addressed in reasoning.

difficulty_calibration.notes: The pre-existing depression and personality changes add moderate complexity by potentially delaying diagnosis. Word-finding difficulty in a temporal lobe lesion is a good clinical correlation. Appropriate for moderate difficulty.

demographic_plausibility.notes: 52F with HTN, hypothyroidism, depression. GBM at age 52 is within expected range. Female adds demographic diversity.

educational_value.notes: Good teaching case highlighting how pre-existing psychiatric conditions can mask brain tumor symptoms. Left temporal location with aphasia adds clinical-anatomic correlation value.

strengths: Left temporal location with language dysfunction
